rulings.Case Title: THERESA LYNNE MURPHY VS. SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY ASSESOR-RECORDER, PHIL TING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of, And Motion For, Order To Show Cause For Direct Comtempt Under Ccp 1209(A) Against Andrea M. Hicks, Joseph Poppen, And Bryan Cave Llp
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 26, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF THERESA MURPHY Motion For Order To Show Cause For Direct Comtempt Under CCP 1209(A) Against Andrea M. Hicks, Joseph Poppen, And Bryan Cave LLP. The motion is denied. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate beyond a reasonable doubt that the defendants/counsel violated a court order. Plaintiff fails to present a proper basis for sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PRO UNLIMITED INC. VS. INTEGRITEAM INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Ro Cross-Complaints By All Cross Complainants
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 26, 2015, Line 12, CROSS DEFENDANT PRO UNLIMITED INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication Ro Cross-Complaints By All Cross Complainants. Cross-Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment is granted. First, Cross-Complainants' contentions that the existence of an agency and/or joint employment relationship give rise to an implied contractual obligation on the part of Cross-Defendant to pay Cross-Complainants directly under certain circumstances after having already making payments to Integriteam is neither supported by law nor the evidence presented. None of the facts here give rise to an inference that Cross-Defendant agreed to pay Cross-Complainants directly, particularly in the event that Cross-Defendant had already paid Integriteam. Second, Cross-Complainants cannot recover under a quantum meruit theory because Cross-Defendant has not been unjustly enriched, but instead has made payments for the services rendered by Cross-Complainants to Integriteam as contractually agreed by all parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RODD NOLAN JOSEPH et al VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ETAL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, January 26, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment. Per order of the presiding judge, the motion is assigned to be heard by Judge Kahn because of the CCP 170.6 challenge to Judge Goldsmith. The hearing on this motion will start promptly at 9:00 a.m. (not 9:30 a.m.) in Department 302. Judge Kahn issues the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied. Defendant failed to maintain its burden of production. Defendant does not show that it is entitled to immunity pursuant to Government Code sec. 855.4 or the other cited sections because defendant does not establish that it carried out with due care its policies regarding the transmission of Legionella baccilus. One of defendant's policies with regard to preventing the transmission of Legionella baccilus is to refrain from using showers or tubs in bone marrow transplant rooms. (Amy Nichols Depo., 43:23-44:2; Locelia Purdy Depo., 48:6-25). The declaration of Bruce Mace fails to address that safety measure or whether defendant complied with it. The court rejects defendant's causation arguments that are raised for the first time in defendant's reply papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
